{
  "roadmap_5_days": [
    "Session 1-2 drill: Pull up your MCX 8800 CE position log and manually reconstruct the 6+ recycling cycles - entry price, cover price, reload trigger. Then write the exact three numerical conditions (volatility level, premium threshold, spread width) that caused each reload. This is not journaling - this is reverse-engineering your own franchise edge into a standing rule card that sits on your desk before every session. You are not allowed to trade MCX until this card exists.",
    "Session 2-3 drill: Open your broker terminal and locate the setting for order filters or trade limits. Set a hard reject on any BUY order where option price is below Rs1.00. If your broker does not support price-floor filters, call the dealer desk and request a risk parameter override that flags sub-Re1 buys for mandatory callback confirmation. Test it with a dummy order. Confirm it fires. This replaces six months of failed willpower with one five-minute infrastructure change.",
    "Session 4-5 drill: Before market open on each of these two sessions, write exactly three instruments - drawn only from EICHERMOT, BAJFINANCE, and MCX - with specific strike, direction, lot size (score-proportional, no TATASTEEL-scale overrides), and a single exit condition. Execute only these three. Zero entries outside this list until 10:30. After close, record actual execution vs plan and mark every deviation with the word CONTAMINATED. The goal is not perfection - it is building awareness of the exact moment the pre-planned self hands over to the reactive self."
  ],
  "roadmap_30_days": [
    "Expiry-week regime shift: Beginning with the next expiry cycle, designate the 3rd Thursday through Friday as a CLOSE-ONLY regime. No new BUY entries of any kind on expiry day or the day prior. Any open position priced below Rs0.50 is closed at market on the 3rd Wednesday close - not converted, not averaged, not held into expiry hoping for lottery payoff. The mechanics: set a calendar reminder for the 3rd Wednesday at 3:00 PM labeled LOTTERY PREVENTION CLOSE. This directly attacks the Jun 26, Jul 31, Aug 28, Sep 29-30 destruction events which are the single largest identifiable P&L drain in the dataset. The what-if simulation shows the worst 5 trades - predominantly expiry-day lottery buys - account for Rs1,00,789 in recoverable losses.",
    "Score-size coupling regime shift: Build a three-cell spreadsheet that auto-populates before each session. Column A: instrument. Column B: total_score output. Column C: f_score. The formula in Column D outputs one of three words - FULL (f=1, score above 80, t above 1.5), HALF (f=1, score 60-80), or ZERO (f=0 or score below 60). No trade enters the platform without a FULL or HALF tag. TATASTEEL at t=-3.01 and f=0 would have printed ZERO and received no capital. CHOLAFIN at score 94.5 would have printed FULL and received proportional size instead of a Rs0.05 token. This is the direct surgical fix to the score-size decoupling identified as critical - your scoring system has been a dashboard ornament; this makes it a gatekeeper.",
    "FOMO re-entry structural prohibition: Create a session-level trade log with one additional column - EXITED TODAY (yes/no). Any instrument marked YES in that column is locked from new BUY entries until the following session open. CUMMINSIND sold at 11.5 and rebought at 17.5 same day, DIXON sold at 443-489 and repurchased at 520-521 thirty-six minutes later, BOSCHLTD sold at 121-123 and rebought at 140-150 same session - these are not independent decisions, they are a single behavioral cascade triggered by regret at a profitable exit. The prohibition does not require you to understand the psychology in the moment; it requires only that you check one column before clicking BUY. Estimated direct recovery from eliminating revenge re-entries: Rs53,276 per simulation, with secondary benefit of freeing capital for the overnight theta book where your 84.8% win rate operates."
  ],
  "one_move_to_change_everything": "Install a hard broker-level price floor that rejects any BUY order on an option priced below Rs1.00, effective immediately and permanently, with no override capability available during market hours. This single structural block eliminates the entire expiry-cycle lottery ritual - Jun 26, Jul 31, Aug 28, Sep 29-30 - which is the only behavior with a near-100% loss rate and the only behavior that repeats without exception regardless of prior month results, market regime, or portfolio state. The what-if simulation confirms removing the worst 5 trades alone recovers Rs1,00,789, a 13.3% P&L uplift, and those worst trades are concentrated in exactly this sub-Re1 expiry-day lottery category. Combined with the existing 84.8% overnight win rate and Rs8,06,102 overnight P&L already being generated, blocking the lottery subsidy loop stops the single mechanism that drains disciplined theta gains back to zero every month.",
  "one_move_rationale": "The forensic data shows two personas operating one account. The premium-selling persona generates Rs8,06,102 overnight with an 84.8% win rate - this is not a broken strategy, it is a functioning edge. The lottery persona generates near-zero wins on Rs0.05 OTM options and has repeated identically across every expiry in the dataset with no learning curve and no degradation of frequency. Willpower, awareness, and reflection have all been present - the trader clearly understands this pattern exists - and none have stopped it across six documented expiry cycles. This means the intervention cannot be psychological; it must be architectural. A broker-level price floor below Rs1.00 is the only intervention that removes the instrument of the compulsion before the compulsive state activates. The trader in lottery mode cannot override a system-level reject. The simulations show Rs1,00,789 recoverable from the worst 5 trades alone, but the structural value is larger: stopping the monthly capital-destruction event also stops the cognitive contamination effect where lottery-mode decision quality bleeds into the broader book during expiry sessions, amplifying fragmentation and adverse covers across positions that would otherwise be managed competently. This is not the highest-uplift simulation (the win-rate improvement at Rs2,97,791 is larger in magnitude) but it is the only one achievable through a single five-minute infrastructure action rather than a multi-month behavioral change program."
}
